last_item: DatabaseTuning — page 'reads as prescriptive when it's really informational' -> add one reassuring intro line (defaults already tuned for the hardware; the typical indie app needs no changes). med/M, proposed as intro-only S.
last_result: REJECTED on restraint (the loop's 5th Mode-F reject; iter-5 pattern -- premise FALSE on inspection, self-rejected with decisive code evidence, NO panel). The reassurance the item proposes ALREADY EXISTS on the page threefold: (1) the card title is 'Database tuning (host-sized)' -- 'host-sized' = automatic; (2) the very first element is an info Callout titled 'Sized to this server automatically' whose body reads 'Postgres is tuned to this machine on safe best defaults -- you don't need to tune anything by hand' -- that IS the proposed intro line, verbatim in spirit; (3) the active Mixed profile's own description reads 'the best default for an indie-hacker box', telling the indie user their profile is already right (and the profile preview is explicitly framed 'This is a preview -- nothing changes here ... applied at install/provision time -- not from this screen'). The item's second sub-concern ('help text assumes DBA knowledge: shared_buffers/work_mem') is also already handled -- each setting carries a plain-English one-liner (e.g. 'Memory Postgres uses to cache data pages. Sized to your RAM.') -- and the proposed fix didn't address it anyway. There is no edit that wouldn't restate a reassurance the page already gives, so unlike iters 7/13 (plausible changes 3 reviewers shipped) there was nothing to put before a panel -- running 4 agents to rubber-stamp a provably-zero-payoff change is the churn the loop guards against (iter-5 precedent). Made no code change; recorded the lesson ('when an item says add a reassuring/intro line, read the view's existing intro FIRST -- an audit agent skimming the parameter table can miss the Callout right above it'); marked the backlog item rejected. Docs-only commit, no hard gates needed. BACKLOG NOW NEAR-DRY: only a NEEDS-BACKEND item (backup-badge health, out of scope for this frontend loop) and two low/watch items (Query client-side write-detector -- speculative/restraint-risky; Login lockout copy -- risks a false duration promise) remain. Next iteration: run a Mode-S discovery/convergence check rather than chew the low/watch items; if it surfaces no high/med item, that's the FIRST stable_streak increment toward convergence (>=3 -> write COMPLETE.md).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn -- rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres -- minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
